free_text: The 39K(alpha,p)42Ca cross section and thermonuclear
reaction rate
The University of Melbourne 5U Pelletron
Protons were detected by means of a 100 mm**2
surface barrier detector located 8.8 cm from the target
in the  125 degree c.m. direction, and covered with an
Al foil of thickness 50 mu-m.
A 60 cm**3 Ge(Li) detector was mounted 3 cm from
the target in the 55 degree direction. The function of
this detector was to provide confirmatory evidence for
identification of proton peaks in the (alpha,p) spectra
A target of KBr,enriched to 99.96%
in 39K,was prepared by evaporation of KBr from a
previously outgassed tungsten boat on to a 0.13 mm
thick gold backing which had been etched in aqua regia,
rinsed in doubly deionized water,and outgassed.
The target thickness was
Scale error. The main contributors being:
* target thickness, 6%,
* The solid angle, 4%,
* The beam current integration, 4%.
